steps.title: Identify what the underlined portion does
steps.content: The underlined phrase "(a chemical compound usable as a detergent)" appears in parentheses immediately after the word "surfactant." Parenthetical phrases set off this way typically define or explain the term they follow. The question asks which choice best states that function.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — It clarifies the meaning of a scientific term.: the phrase defines "surfactant" in plain language (a chemical compound usable as a detergent), which is exactly clarifying the meaning of a scientific term. ✓
  • Choice B — It describes an environmental concern.: the phrase says nothing about environmental problems; it only defines what a surfactant is — this misidentifies the function. ✗
  • Choice C — It explains the significance of a scientific discovery.: the phrase does not assess why the discovery matters; it simply labels what a surfactant is — this misidentifies the function. ✗
  • Choice D — It identifies a result that confused the team.: nothing in the passage says the team was confused, and the phrase does not identify a result — this introduces a detail absent from the text. ✗
That is the correct answer.
